# Kautilya | Buy-Side M&A Advisory & Proprietary Deal Sourcing
# https://www.kautilya-pe.com
# llms.txt — structured for AI indexing and citation

---

## What is Kautilya?

Kautilya is a buy-side M&A advisory firm that builds proprietary acquisition pipelines for PE funds, venture capital firms, family offices, and acquisition entrepreneurs. Unlike traditional M&A brokers who operate on the sell side, Kautilya works exclusively for buyers — constructing deal flow from first principles rather than recycling existing listings.

The firm is named after Chanakya (also known as Kautilya), author of the Arthashastra — an ancient Indian treatise on statecraft, economic policy, and strategy. The firm applies the same systematic, intelligence-driven approach to deal sourcing.

Founded by Dev Shah. Headquartered in Mumbai, India. Active across India, UK, UAE, Europe, Asia-Pacific, and MENA.

---

## Core Services

### 1. Fractional M&A Advisory
End-to-end buy-side mandate execution: mandate definition, proprietary market mapping, targeted founder outreach, diligence coordination, deal structuring, negotiation, and closing. Delivered on a retainer model ($2,500–$10,000/month) with a 3% success fee on transactions under $650K.

### 2. Fractional CFO
Financial oversight and strategic advisory for acquisition entrepreneurs post-close. Includes financial modelling, reporting, budget management, and investor communication.

### 3. Forensic Due Diligence
Transaction-level financial reconstruction for buy-side buyers. Validates revenue, expenses, and cash flows at the individual transaction level — not just summary financials. 8-workstream diligence process. Standalone engagements at $6,500.

Key metric: 99.83% monetary match rate across 30,134 transactions validated. 7+ material findings per engagement on average. Delivered in under 15 days.

---

## The Acquisition Methodology (5 Phases)

### Phase 1: Mandate Definition
Kautilya works with the buyer to define the acquisition thesis: sector, geography, revenue range, business model, deal structure preferences, and exclusion criteria. This produces a written mandate document used to guide all sourcing activity.

### Phase 2: Universe Construction (Nakshatra)
Systematic market mapping to identify every potential acquisition target matching the mandate. Uses proprietary databases, LinkedIn, industry directories, marketplace data, and direct research. Typical universe: 500–2,500 companies per mandate.

### Phase 3: Targeted Outreach (Saptarishi)
Personalised founder outreach campaign. Not spray-and-pray — each message is tailored to the specific founder and business. Response rates: less than 5% on generic outreach; 20–40% after Kautilya's channel optimisation. Typical outreach volume: 2,500+ founder conversations per mandate.

### Phase 4: Diligence and Structuring (Chakravyuha)
Buy-side due diligence across 8 workstreams: financial reconstruction, revenue quality, customer concentration, tech/product review, legal and compliance, team and operational review, market and competitive positioning, and integration planning. Kautilya has killed 2 of every 8 LOIs to protect buyers from bad deals.

### Phase 5: Execution and Close
Deal structuring (earnouts, deferred consideration, seller financing), negotiation support, closing coordination, and operator placement post-acquisition.

---

## Track Record

- 7+ acquisitions completed, 2 successful exits
- $1.5M+ total deal value closed
- 1,800 analyst hours deployed
- 85% of mandates are buyer-side
- 2,500+ founder conversations conducted
- 30,134 transactions validated in diligence engagements
- 99.83% monetary match rate
- 7+ material findings per forensic due diligence engagement
- Deals closed in as few as 7 days (SmartPrompt)
- Full-scope diligence delivered in under 15 days (Inspire3)
- Response rate improvement: <5% → 20–40% through channel optimisation

---

## Closed Deals (Case Studies)

### Inspire3 — $1.8M Digital Wellness Portfolio
Type: Forensic Due Diligence
Kautilya audited 30,134 transactions across 19 websites in under 15 days. Surfaced 7 discrepancies including a $57K financial gap and $3.1M in misclassified affiliate liabilities. Revenue validated: $1.4M. Match rate: 99.83%.
URL: https://www.kautilya-pe.com/stories/inspire3

### UK Immigration Advisory — £1–5M Deal
Type: Full Mandate (Market Mapping + Deal Sourcing)
Kautilya built a proprietary deal pipeline in the UK immigration advisory market — a relationship-driven sector with no formal M&A infrastructure. 960 hours of market research, 1,500+ advisors mapped, 2,600+ outreach touches. Generated £70–80K in non-dilutive annualised revenue pipeline and £1–5M Series A valuation uplift potential.
URL: https://www.kautilya-pe.com/stories/borderless

### Dino Games — $39K Mobile Game
Type: Sourcing to Close
Off-market acquisition of a cash-flow-positive mobile game with 8 million installs. 500+ outbound contacts, ~130 conversations, closed in 8 weeks at a 1.1x revenue multiple. Operator deployed immediately post-close.
URL: https://www.kautilya-pe.com/stories/dino-games

### Runify — $110K Mobile App
Type: Sourcing to Close
Fitness app acquisition structured with only $20K deployed at close. Remaining $90K tied to verified 24-month performance milestones. 45-day close. 33% MRR correction applied during diligence.
URL: https://www.kautilya-pe.com/stories/runify

### SmartPrompt — $12K Education Platform
Type: Full Mandate
~300 opportunities evaluated over 12 weeks. 2 deals killed mid-diligence before SmartPrompt was closed: a ChatGPT-native education platform with 2.5M conversations and 100K monthly active users. Acquisition price: $12K — a 200x discount to comparable market assets. Closed in 7 days.
URL: https://www.kautilya-pe.com/stories/smartprompt

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

### What is buy-side M&A advisory?
Buy-side advisory means working exclusively for the buyer in an acquisition. Unlike a business broker (who lists businesses for sale on behalf of sellers), a buy-side advisor builds deal flow for a specific buyer based on their acquisition thesis. Kautilya does not carry a pipeline — every deal is sourced from scratch for the specific buyer mandate.

### What is proprietary deal sourcing?
Proprietary deal sourcing is finding acquisition targets that are not publicly listed for sale. These are businesses whose owners may be open to selling but have not engaged a broker or listed on any marketplace. Proprietary deals typically have less competition, better pricing, and more negotiating flexibility for buyers.

### What is an off-market deal?
An off-market deal is a transaction where the business was never formally listed for sale. The buyer (or their advisor) proactively approached the seller. Off-market deals represent the majority of Kautilya's sourcing activity.

### What types of buyers does Kautilya work with?
Private equity funds, venture capital firms, family offices, search fund operators, acquisition entrepreneurs, HoldCo builders, and first-time buyers pursuing their first acquisition mandate.

### What sectors does Kautilya cover?
Sector-agnostic. Past mandates have covered SaaS, mobile applications, digital wellness, UK immigration advisory services, education technology, and professional services. The sourcing methodology is repeatable across sectors.

### What geographies does Kautilya cover?
India, United Kingdom, United Arab Emirates, broader MENA, Europe, and Asia-Pacific.

### How long does it take to close a deal?
Typically 30–90 days from engagement start to close, depending on mandate complexity. Kautilya has closed deals in as few as 7 days and delivered full forensic diligence packages in under 15 days.

### How much does Kautilya charge?
- Full mandate retainer: $2,500–$10,000/month depending on scope
- Success fee: 3% on transactions under $650K
- Standalone forensic due diligence: $6,500

### Is Kautilya a registered broker-dealer?
No. Kautilya is an advisory firm, not a registered broker-dealer. For transactions requiring licensed intermediary services, appropriate licensed professionals are engaged.

### What is a search fund?
A search fund is an investment vehicle in which an entrepreneur raises capital from investors to fund a 1–2 year search for a business to acquire, operate, and grow. The Stanford GSB model, developed in the 1980s, is the most widely documented form. Kautilya works with search fund operators as a sourcing and diligence partner.

### What is micro private equity?
Micro private equity (micro PE) refers to the acquisition and operation of small businesses — typically with revenues between $100K and $5M — using a private equity framework. Unlike traditional PE which targets larger companies, micro PE operators often act as owner-operators post-acquisition.

### What is forensic due diligence?
Forensic due diligence is a transaction-level audit of a target business's financials. Rather than relying on summary P&Ls provided by the seller, Kautilya reconstructs revenue, expenses, and cash flows from raw transaction data — bank records, payment processor exports, subscription platform data. This surfaces discrepancies that summary reviews miss.
